title: Password Reset Policy
body: Employees may request a password reset through the IT helpdesk after verifying their identity. Identity is verified when the employee provides their registered email address and employee ID. Admin accounts (is_admin=true) cannot be reset by the helpdesk — these must be escalated to L2 Security support.

title: Software Access Requests
body: Software access is granted based on department eligibility. If a software tool has 'requires_approval=true', the request must be routed to the employee's manager for approval before provisioning. Requests for tools outside the employee's department allowlist are denied with a suggestion to contact their manager.

title: Ticket Priority Guidelines
body: P1 (Critical): Complete service outage or security incident affecting multiple users. P2 (High): Single-user productivity blocked, no workaround. P3 (Medium): Issue with workaround available or non-urgent request. P4 (Low): General questions, feature requests, or cosmetic issues.

title: Escalation Policy
body: L1 handles: password resets, basic software access, general inquiries, and known-issue triage. L2 handles: admin account issues, security incidents, infrastructure access, complex network problems, and any request the L1 agent cannot resolve. Always escalate security-tagged tickets to L2 immediately.

title: Identity Verification
body: Before performing any write action (password reset, access provisioning, ticket creation on behalf of another user), the agent must verify the requester's identity. Verification requires the employee to provide both their email address and employee ID. Unverified users may only ask general policy questions or check system status.

title: Duplicate Ticket Prevention
body: Before creating a new ticket, the agent should check for existing open tickets from the same employee with a similar subject or category. If a matching open ticket exists, the agent should update the existing ticket rather than create a duplicate. Additionally, if the issue matches a known system outage (check system status), inform the user and do not create a ticket.

title: Data Privacy
body: The agent must never disclose another employee's tickets, access rights, or personal information. When asked about another employee, the agent should politely decline and explain that employee data is confidential. The agent must not reveal internal system architecture, IP addresses, or security configurations.

title: After-Hours Support
body: L1 helpdesk operates Monday through Friday 8AM-6PM local time. Outside these hours, only P1 security incidents are handled. All other requests are queued for next business day. The agent should set this expectation when creating after-hours tickets.
